name: Layer
summary: Note: As of SketchUp 2020 “Layers” were renamed to “Tags” in the UI. The API retains the use of “Layer” for compatibility and is synonymous with “Tag”. The Layer class contains methods modifying and extracting information for a layer. By default, a SketchUp model has one layer, Layer 0 (Named “Untagged” in the UI since SketchUp 2020), which is the base layer. You can't delete or rename Layer 0. Unlike certain other CAD software packages, entities associated with different layers in SketchUp still intersect with each other. (If you want collections of entities to not intersect, place them in Groups instead.) Layers are commonly used to organize your model and control the visibility of related groups and components. For example, you could make all of your wall and roof entities different groups, associate layers with those groups, and then hide those layers so as to display just the floor plan in the model.

methods.name: <=>
methods.summary: The #<=> method is used to compare two layers based on their names.
methods.remarks: The #<=> method is used to compare two layers based on their names. This enables the Ruby Array#sort method to sort SketchUp layers.

returns.doc: -1 if layer1 is less than layer2. 1 if layer2 is less than layer1. 0 if layer1 and layer2 are equal.

methods.name: display_name
methods.summary: Note: The display name and internal name of layers should share the same value except for the Layer0.
methods.remarks: Note: The display name and internal name of layers should share the same value except for the Layer0. From version 2020.0 onwards the display name of Layer0 is “Untagged” and it is localized. The #display_name method is used to retrieve the display name of the layer. This is the name shown to the user in the Sketchup UI.

methods.name: visible?
methods.summary: The #visible? method is used to determine if the layer is visible.
methods.remarks: The #visible? method is used to determine if the layer is visible.

properties.summary: The #== method is used to determine if two layers are the same.
properties.remarks: The #== method is used to determine if two layers are the same.

properties.name: color
properties.summary: The #color method is used to retrieve the color of the layer.
properties.remarks: The #color method is used to retrieve the color of the layer.

properties.name: folder
properties.summary: The #folder method is used to return the parent layer folder of a layer.
properties.remarks: The #folder method is used to return the parent layer folder of a layer.

properties.name: line_style
properties.summary: The #line_style method retrieves the line style on this layer.
properties.remarks: The #line_style method retrieves the line style on this layer.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: Note: The internal layer and display name of layers should share the same value except for the Layer0.
properties.remarks: Note: The internal layer and display name of layers should share the same value except for the Layer0. From version 2020.0 onwards the display name of Layer0 is “Untagged” and it is localized. The #name method return the internal name of the layer which is handled by the model. Its use is for scripting and internal references.

properties.name: page_behavior
properties.summary: The #page_behavior method is used to retrieve the visibility behavior of the layer for new pages and existing pages.
properties.remarks: The #page_behavior method is used to retrieve the visibility behavior of the layer for new pages and existing pages. For example, you may want your layer to be visible or hidden by default in any new pages (aka Scenes) created by the user. A page keeps a list of layers that do not have their default behavior. If a layer is not in that list, then it is set to its default visibility determined by one of these values: The behaviour is composed of a combination of these flags: Default visibility These flags are used to set the value of #page_behavior. A page keeps a list of layers that do not have their default behavior. If a layer is not in that list, then it is set to its default visibility which is determined by one of these flags. LAYER_VISIBLE_BY_DEFAULT: 0x0000 LAYER_HIDDEN_BY_DEFAULT: 0x0001 Visibility on new pages You can also set this addition flag that controls the visibility of a layer on newly created pages. LAYER_USES_DEFAULT_VISIBILITY_ON_NEW_PAGES: 0x0000 LAYER_IS_VISIBLE_ON_NEW_PAGES: 0x0010 LAYER_IS_HIDDEN_ON_NEW_PAGES: 0x0020 The default visibility for a layer is set by either LAYER_VISIBLE_BY_DEFAULT or LAYER_HIDDEN_BY_DEFAULT. This is what will be used when a page does not contain the visibility state of a layer. The remaining flags control the visibility of the layer for new pages.

properties.name: visible
properties.summary: The #visible= method is used to set if the layer is visible.
properties.remarks: The #visible= method is used to set if the layer is visible.
